overview: Affinity Stompy in Legacy leverages artifact ramp and cost reduction to deploy a wide board of resilient creatures quickly. The U/R color scheme provides instant-speed interaction and card selection to protect your game plan against aggressive and control opponents. By prioritizing early artifact drops, you accelerate into a dominant mid-game where reduced costs enable overwhelming combat pressure.
win_conditions: Deploying multiple low-cost artifact creatures to overwhelm opponents with combat damage
Using artifact count to reduce creature costs and accelerate ahead of opponent's curve
Maintaining board presence through U/R instant-speed interaction while applying relentless pressure

keep_criteria: Hands containing at least two lands and an early artifact or creature
Draws with Urza's Bauble or Lotus Petal alongside a one-drop creature or removal
Hands with Mox Opal and a land that can produce blue or red mana
Keep hands with 2+ mana sources (Mox Opal, Lotus Petal, Urza's Bauble, or basic lands) and a turn-1 or turn-2 threat/playable artifact.
Prioritize hands that can cast turn-1 spells or accelerate into turn-2 threats.
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking lands or early plays
Draws with only high-cost creatures and no ramp
Hands missing artifact synergy or U/R interaction
Mulligan hands with 3+ lands, no artifacts, or only expensive threats.

early_game: Play Urza's Bauble and Lotus Petal on turn one to establish immediate mana acceleration
Drop early artifact creatures to begin reducing costs and applying pressure
Use U/R cantrips or removal to find lands and protect early threats
Turn 1: Play a land, cast Mox Opal/Lotus Petal/Urza's Bauble.
If holding multiple artifacts, sacrifice Urza's Bauble for mana to cast a cheap threat or spell.
Turn 2: Deploy Arcbound Ravager, Myr Enforcer, or Manamorphose.
Sequence artifacts to maximize Opal triggers and generate mana for threats.
mid_game: Leverage Mox Opal for color fixing and additional mana to play multiple creatures per turn
Prioritize wide artifact creature drops to maximize cost reduction and board presence
Sequence removal to clear blockers while maintaining tempo
Don't overextend into sweepers or removal.
Use artifacts to generate mana efficiently but keep a reserve for interaction.
Manage graveyard carefully if facing graveyard hate.
Commit threats when you have protection or can trade favorably.
Hold back if opponent has sweepers or hard removal.
Use artifacts to trade into removal or stabilize.
late_game: Stabilize with a wide board of artifact creatures that are difficult to clear efficiently
Use U/R interaction to counter or remove opponent's finishers or board wipes
Push for lethal by playing multiple reduced-cost creatures in a single turn
Arcbound Ravager with +1/+1 counters, Manamorphose, Myr Enforcer, or artifact combos.
Win through combat or artifact synergy.
When you have multiple artifacts on board, opponent has no sweepers, and you can close the game in 1-2 turns.

key_cards.name: Mox Opal
key_cards.notes: Play early to enable U/R spells and accelerate artifact creature drops; prioritize when you need specific colored mana for interaction

key_cards.name: Lotus Petal
key_cards.notes: Deploy on turn one to enable turn-two plays; sacrifice or use carefully to maintain artifact count for cost reduction

key_cards.name: Urza's Bauble
key_cards.notes: Provides early mana and contributes to artifact count; use its ability to tap for colorless mana to play creatures ahead of curve

sequencing_tips: Play artifacts before creatures to maximize cost reduction for subsequent drops
Hold U/R instant-speed interaction in hand to protect key artifact creature plays
Prioritize Lotus Petal and Urza's Bauble on turns one and two to establish early board presence
Use Mox Opal flexibly to fix mana for removal or to accelerate into a wide turn
Common mistake: overcommitting to the board without protection.
Unique interaction: Mox Opal triggers can be used to pay for costs or generate mana for threats.
Watch out for Null Rod and Rest in Peace.

matchups.advice: Play aggressively around potential board wipes; prioritize artifact creatures that can return from the graveyard or are resilient to sweepers. Use U/R interaction to disrupt their late-game setup.
Stabilize early by playing efficient artifact creatures and using U/R removal to clear their threats. Leverage cost reduction to outpace their damage output.
Disrupt their combo pieces with U/R interaction and apply immediate pressure to force them to respond. Prioritize artifact creatures that can block or trade efficiently.
Race them by going wide with artifact creatures; use cost reduction to play multiple threats per turn. Hold interaction for their key removal or sweepers.
Apply early pressure before they establish a large mana base; use U/R interaction to disrupt their ramp pieces. Leverage artifact count to accelerate ahead of their curve.
Use cheap, multiple threats (cantrips, artifact tokens, or split the cast). Don't rely on single big spells. Use artifacts to generate mana for protection or follow-up.
Win the artifact race, track their interaction, and out-value them with better sequencing or sideboard cards.

common_threats: Board wipes that clear artifact creatures
Watch for counterspells (Flusterstorm, Ponder/Preordain sequencing), sweepers (Pyroclasm, Wrath), and graveyard hate.
Note when opponents tap out for interaction.
